data.title: Swan Corp Seeks Shareholder Nod via E-Vote for New Independent Directors
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Swan Corp ( (IN:SWANCORP) ) has issued an announcement.

Swan Corp Limited has initiated a postal ballot process to seek shareholder approval for the appointment of Ms. Bhagwati Sharma and Mr. Ashish Chhabria as non-executive independent directors. The company has dispatched the postal ballot notice electronically to eligible shareholders, who can cast their votes exclusively through remote e-voting between April 20 and May 19, 2026.

The voting process is being conducted in compliance with the Companies Act, SEBI listing regulations and MCA guidelines, with NSDL engaged to provide the e-voting platform and an external scrutinizer appointed to oversee the ballot. Results are expected on or before May 21, 2026, and the board changes, if approved, would further strengthen Swan Corp’s governance framework and independent oversight for investors.

More about Swan Corp

Swan Corp Limited, formerly known as Swan Energy Limited, is an Indian listed company with roots dating back to 1909 and a registered office in Mumbai’s Ballard Estate. The company is listed on both the National Stock Exchange of India and BSE, and it communicates with shareholders through digital channels and stock exchange filings in line with SEBI’s disclosure norms.

Average Trading Volume: 84,992

Technical Sentiment Signal: Hold

Current Market Cap: 104.3B INR

data.title: JAPEX Warns Middle East Tensions to Raise LNG Costs and Hit Iraq Oil Revenue
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Japan Petroleum Exploration Co ( (JP:1662) ) has provided an update.

Japan Petroleum Exploration Co. says escalating tensions in the Middle East and a de facto blockade of the Strait of Hormuz have forced it to procure two first-quarter fiscal 2026 LNG cargoes on the spot market instead of from the Persian Gulf. This substitution is expected to significantly raise LNG procurement costs, although the company stresses that alternative arrangements mean there is no immediate risk to natural gas or power supply for its Japanese customers.

The company’s participation in Iraq’s Garraf oil field has also been hit, with production and shipments suspended following a force majeure declaration by the Iraqi government and no visibility on resumption, eliminating expected revenue from the project. While higher crude prices and a weaker yen are supporting revenue, JAPEX warns that unplanned LNG spot purchases, the Garraf shutdown and tight supply of chemical products for domestic fields could sharply erode profits, with detailed impacts to be reflected in its fiscal 2027 forecast, though effects on fiscal 2026 results are expected to be minimal.

JAPEX is analyzing the financial implications of these disruptions and will update its earnings outlook once it can better gauge the trajectory of regional tensions and supply-chain conditions. The company says it will continue monitoring developments closely while prioritizing stable energy supply despite cost pressures and operational uncertainty in the Middle East.

The most recent analyst rating on (JP:1662) stock is a Hold with a Yen980.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Japan Petroleum Exploration Co stock, see the JP:1662 Stock Forecast page.

More about Japan Petroleum Exploration Co

Japan Petroleum Exploration Co., Ltd. is a Japanese energy company engaged in exploration, production and procurement of oil and natural gas, including liquefied natural gas used as feedstock for gas supply and as fuel for power generation. The company also operates and supplies electricity through assets such as the Fukushima Natural Gas Power Plant and participates in overseas upstream projects like the Garraf oil field in Iraq.

Average Trading Volume: 3,793,833

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Buy

Current Market Cap: Yen613.3B

data.title: VietNam Holding Flags Tough March but Welcomes Vietnam’s FTSE Upgrade
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An announcement from Vietnam Holding ( (GB:VNH) ) is now available.

VietNam Holding Limited reported that March was a difficult month for Vietnamese equities, with the fund’s net asset value falling 10%, though it still outperformed its benchmark thanks to a focus on quality companies with resilient earnings. The manager highlighted that, despite geopolitical tensions in the Middle East and rising energy costs, Vietnam is showing resilience, underpinned by strong foreign direct investment, proactive energy security measures, and confirmation that FTSE Russell will upgrade the country to Secondary Emerging Market status in September, a move expected to catalyse future capital inflows and further develop its capital markets.

Vietnam’s modest uptick in inflation reflects higher energy prices, but the impact is tempered by a relatively less energy‑intensive economy and domestic capacity in hydropower, oil and gas, renewables and agriculture. Political developments, including new leadership for the prime minister and presidency, are seen as reinforcing policy continuity and stability, while rising imports of machinery and intermediate goods suggest a pipeline for stronger exports later in the year, supporting Vietnam’s long‑term growth narrative despite near‑term market volatility.

Spark’s Take on VNH Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, VNH is a Neutral.

The score is held down primarily by highly volatile earnings and cash flows, including a downturn to losses and negative free cash flow in 2025. Technicals add additional pressure with bearish trend signals (price below key moving averages and negative MACD). The balance sheet provides a stabilizing positive with zero debt, but the negative P/E and lack of dividend yield data limit valuation support.

To see Spark’s full report on VNH stock, click here.

More about Vietnam Holding

VietNam Holding Limited is an investment company focused on Vietnam’s equity markets, managed by Dynam Capital Limited. The fund targets Vietnamese companies benefiting from the country’s structural growth, increased foreign direct investment and ongoing integration into global supply chains.

data.title: Pensana secures US$165m Cascade backing for Longonjo rare earth expansion and U.S. mine-to-magnet push
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An update from Pensana Rare Earths PLC ( (GB:PRE) ) is now available.

Pensana has secured the first US$15 million tranche of a planned US$165 million strategic investment from Cascade Natural Resources, which will take a 3.8% stake in Pensana and a 38.2% holding in its Longonjo mine subsidiary, Sable. Together with a proposed US$160 million debt package, the funding is expected to fully finance construction of the Longonjo mine, expansion drilling toward a one-billion-tonne resource and a heavy rare earth recovery circuit, underpinning Pensana’s plan to establish a U.S.-focused, mine-to-magnet supply chain amid growing pressure to diversify away from Chinese rare earth dominance.

Cascade, backed by specialist family offices and sovereign investors and chaired by former JP Morgan metals and mining head Lloyd Pengilly, will also gain board representation at Pensana, strengthening governance and strategic backing. The issue of nearly 14 million new shares at 80 pence will lift Pensana’s share count to 353.5 million, while first production from Longonjo is targeted for 2027, positioning the project as a significant new source of magnet metals for global electrification and energy transition markets.

Spark’s Take on PRE Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, PRE is a Neutral.

The score is held back primarily by weak financial fundamentals (no revenue, widening losses, and negative free cash flow) alongside a rising debt load. Technicals are moderately supportive with improving trend/momentum, but valuation signals remain weak due to continued losses and the absence of dividend support.

To see Spark’s full report on PRE stock, click here.

More about Pensana Rare Earths PLC

Pensana Plc is a rare earth mining and processing company focused on developing the Longonjo rare earth mine in Angola to produce mixed rare earth carbonate containing both light and heavy magnet metals. The company aims to build a fully integrated U.S.-aligned mine-to-magnet supply chain, exporting product via the Lobito rail corridor and partnering with U.S. industry and government stakeholders.

Average Trading Volume: 673,140

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: £297.1M

VinaCapital Vietnam Opportunity Fund Limited AccumUSD ( (GB:VOF) ) has shared an update.

VinaCapital Vietnam Opportunity Fund Limited has repurchased 30,854 US dollar-denominated ordinary shares at GBP 4.73415 per share, with the shares to be held in treasury as part of its ongoing capital management strategy. Following this transaction, the fund holds 6,515,174 shares in treasury, leaving 123,394,485 shares in issue and voting rights outstanding, a figure that will guide investors’ regulatory disclosure calculations under UK transparency rules.

The update also confirms that the total voting rights now stand at 123,394,485, a key reference point for shareholders monitoring their notifiable stakes under the FCA’s Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules. By tightening its free float through buybacks while maintaining regulatory clarity on voting rights, the fund may enhance net asset value per share and support liquidity management for existing investors in the Vietnam-focused vehicle.

data.title: SThree Cancels 206,679 Shares After Latest Buyback Tranche
data.description:

The latest announcement is out from SThree plc ( (GB:STEM) ).

SThree plc has repurchased a total of 206,679 ordinary shares over the period from 9 to 15 April 2026, executing the buybacks on the London Stock Exchange at volume-weighted average prices ranging from about 148p to 154p. The company plans to cancel these shares, which will reduce the overall share count and may enhance earnings per share and capital returns for investors, signalling continued balance-sheet strength and confidence in its long-term prospects.

The transactions were executed through Investec Bank under SThree’s ongoing share buyback programme, in line with regulatory requirements under the UK Market Abuse Regulation. By continuing to deploy capital into buybacks and cancelling the acquired stock, SThree reinforces a shareholder-friendly capital allocation policy that could support its market valuation and appeal to income and total-return-focused shareholders.

The most recent analyst rating on (GB:STEM) stock is a Buy with a £250.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on SThree plc stock, see the GB:STEM Stock Forecast page.

The score is driven primarily by resilient cash generation and a still-manageable balance sheet, partially offset by a notable recent deterioration in revenue and margins. data.title: GSK Adds 330,000 Shares to Treasury in Ongoing Buyback
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest update is out from GlaxoSmithKline ( (GB:GSK) ).

GSK has continued its ongoing share buyback programme, repurchasing 330,000 ordinary shares on 16 April 2026 through BNP Paribas at a volume-weighted average price of 2,125.06 pence. The shares will be held in treasury, lifting its treasury stock to 259,417,615 shares and leaving 4,056,773,154 shares in issue, with treasury shares now representing 6.39% of voting rights.

Since the launch of its current buyback in February, GSK has acquired 19,526,521 shares, underlining a sustained capital return and balance-sheet management strategy. The updated voting rights figure provides investors with a new reference point for regulatory disclosure thresholds and may support earnings-per-share metrics by reducing the free float over time.

The most recent analyst rating on (GB:GSK) stock is a Hold with a £22.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on GlaxoSmithKline stock, see the GB:GSK Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on GSK Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, GSK is a Neutral.

The score is driven primarily by strong profitability and improving recent fundamentals, supported by constructive 2026 guidance and pipeline momentum. Valuation is reasonable with a modest yield, while the main constraint is technical overbought risk and ongoing balance-sheet/earnings-consistency considerations.

To see Spark’s full report on GSK stock, click here.

Following this latest transaction, the company has bought back a total of 950,712 shares since the programme began, leaving 39,478,535 shares in issue, 3,469,239 held in treasury, and 36,009,296 voting rights in circulation, providing updated reference figures for shareholders’ disclosure obligations.
